refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding latency constraints to the FR to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: All software and hardware components responsible for teleoperation communication management and protocol implementation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics and packet loss statistics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands requiring reliable communication.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation communication logic.
Vehicle module primarily hardware-focused; low containment for communication protocol.
Highest containment and lowest diffusion; best architectural fit for DP implementation.
No direct role in teleoperation communication; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Network Link and Telemetry modules.
Risk of module overload if Teleoperation module scope expands beyond communication.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor Teleoperation module scope to avoid excessive complexity; consider splitting if needed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear mapping and measurable performance.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk due to strong containment in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support the assignment; interfaces are well-defined and limited.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network packet analysis within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for communication quality to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Add latency constraints to FR to balance packet loss and responsiveness.
Review interface definitions for potential enhancements to support dynamic adaptation.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. All gates pass with manageable risks and clear verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: To incorporate latency constraints and ensure balanced teleoperation performance.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Customer-defined packet loss limits are achievable with adaptive protocols.
open_risks: Potential complexity growth in Teleoperation module.
evidence_needed: Empirical packet loss and latency measurements under representative network conditions.
Telemetry data validating communication quality management.
